name: Eleanor Matsuda
chief_complaint: I thought it was just the flu but now I can hardly breathe and I can't stop coughing
onset_description: Developed typical flu symptoms 5 days ago with fever, body aches, and dry cough. Seemed to improve slightly on day 3, but then worsened dramatically on day 4 with productive cough, worsening shortness of breath, and high fever returning.

cough.description: The cough started out dry but now I'm bringing up this thick yellow-green stuff. It hurts my chest every time I cough.
cough.onset: 5 days ago dry, became productive 2 days ago
cough.character: Initially dry, now productive with thick yellow-green sputum

shortness_of_breath.description: I get so winded just walking to the bathroom. Today I could barely get out of bed without feeling like I was suffocating.
shortness_of_breath.character: dyspnea on minimal exertion, now present at rest

fever.description: My temperature went up to 103 last night. I was sweating through my nightgown.
fever.onset: 5 days ago, biphasic pattern (improved then worsened)
fever.character: high fever with rigors, biphasic pattern suggesting secondary infection

pleuritic_chest_pain.description: There's a sharp pain on my right side when I take a deep breath or cough. It catches me.

body_aches.description: My whole body aches, especially my muscles. Even my skin feels sore to touch.
body_aches.location: generalized, worst in back and legs

fatigue.description: I have no energy at all. I feel completely drained. My daughter had to practically carry me to the car.
fatigue.character: profound exhaustion, unable to perform ADLs

decreased_oral_intake.description: I haven't been able to eat much of anything for days. Just sips of water and tea.
decreased_oral_intake.character: poor appetite with minimal fluid intake

labs.CBC: WBC 19.6 (elevated with left shift, 12% bands), Hgb 12.4, Plt 178
labs.BMP: Na 132 (low), K 3.6, Cr 1.4 (elevated from baseline 0.8), BUN 32 (elevated), Glucose 138
labs.sputum_culture: Pending (gram stain shows numerous WBCs, gram-positive diplococci)
labs.ABG: pH 7.44, pCO2 30 (low, hyperventilating), pO2 56 (low), HCO3 21, A-a gradient elevated
labs.chest_xray: Right middle and lower lobe consolidation with air bronchograms, consistent with pneumonia. Small right-sided pleural effusion.

past_medical_history: COPD (moderate, on home oxygen 1L at night only)
medications: Albuterol inhaler 2 puffs every 4-6 hours as needed
Acetaminophen 500mg as needed for arthritis pain

social_history.smoking: Never smoked, but husband smoked heavily (secondhand smoke exposure for 40+ years until his death)
social_history.alcohol: Rare, glass of sake on special occasions
social_history.occupation: Retired schoolteacher, taught elementary school for 35 years
social_history.living_situation: Lives alone in a small house, widowed 6 years ago. Daughter lives 20 minutes away and checks on her daily. Has a home health aide 3 times per week.
social_history.exercise: Light gardening, walks around the house. Limited by arthritis and dyspnea.
social_history.diet: Eats small balanced meals, traditional Japanese diet with rice, fish, and vegetables. Daughter brings meals several times per week.

personality: Gentle, dignified, and stoic. Does not want to be a burden to her daughter. Tends to minimize symptoms. Very polite and formal in interactions. Expresses gratitude frequently even when suffering.
communication_style: Soft-spoken and measured. Uses polite, formal language. May nod and agree even when she doesn't fully understand. Occasionally pauses to catch her breath mid-sentence. Defers difficult questions to her daughter when possible.
pain_description_style: Extremely reluctant to report pain or discomfort. Will say 'it's manageable' or 'I don't want to complain.' Needs very gentle and specific questioning. Cultural tendency to endure pain quietly.

critical_findings: Influenza A with secondary bacterial pneumonia (biphasic illness pattern)
Underlying COPD increasing risk of respiratory failure
Dehydration with acute kidney injury (Cr 1.4 from 0.8)
Age 80 with multiple comorbidities - high mortality risk
Sputum gram stain suggesting Streptococcus pneumoniae superinfection
Did not receive flu vaccine this season (should be assessed)
rubric.diagnosis: Influenza A complicated by secondary bacterial pneumonia (likely Streptococcus pneumoniae)
recommended_interventions: Supplemental oxygen to maintain SpO2 > 92% (titrate carefully with COPD history)
Oseltamivir 75mg BID for 5 days (antiviral for influenza, even late presentation)
IV antibiotics for community-acquired pneumonia (respiratory fluoroquinolone or beta-lactam + macrolide, avoid erythromycin)
IV fluid resuscitation for dehydration and AKI
Serial ABGs or continuous SpO2 monitoring
Consider BiPAP if respiratory status does not improve
CURB-65 score calculation for disposition (likely warrants admission)
Notify close contacts about influenza exposure
